title: Chancela template bundle (v1)
description: A portable, self-describing template unit: the template's JSON spec (props/layout) plus its markdown seed body. Fully portable Chancela-instance to Chancela-instance; partially portable to other tools via this schema and the standard-markdown body. Produced by GET /v1/templates/{id}/export and accepted by POST /v1/templates/import.

format.description: Format discriminator. A body lacking this key is treated by the importer as a legacy bare spec.

format_version.description: Major format version. An importer REJECTS any version it does not implement; it never best-effort transforms a newer bundle.

spec.description: The template spec JSON with its seed removed. The seed rides body_markdown alone, so the two representations can never disagree.

body_markdown.description: The seed body as md-block/v1 markdown: heading-optional, plain-text clauses. Each clause is a section — an optional `## <heading>` line then the clause text, sections separated by a blank line. Plain text only: a seed is rendered as a value and never compiled, so it must contain no minijinja (`{{ }}` / `{% %}`) syntax. Empty when the template has no fillable body. Restricted to the md-block/v1 subset (headings, paragraphs, bold, italic, thematic rules); lists, tables, links, images, and code are rejected on import rather than kept as literal text.

templateSpec.description: A Chancela template spec. The digested render identity of a template; the seed (default_body) is deliberately NOT part of it and is carried by the bundle's body_markdown instead.

id.description: Stable versioned id (e.g. `csc-ata-ag/v1`). A user-authored template must use the reserved `user-<slug>/v<n>` namespace to import.

family.description: The entity family the template serves (e.g. CommercialCompany, Association, Cooperative, Foundation, Condominium).

stage.description: Where in the book lifecycle the template sits (e.g. Ata, TermoAbertura, TermoEncerramento).

channels.description: The meeting channels the template is valid for (e.g. Physical, Remote).

signature_policy.description: The signature policy hint preferred for documents rendered from this template.

rule_pack_id.description: The compliance rule pack bound to the template (the id, not the pack).

blocks.description: The ordered, kind-tagged block layout that produces the rendered document. Each block is a `{kind, ...}` object; the exact per-kind shape is Chancela-internal and intentionally not fully constrained here.

locale.description: The locale the prose is authored in (v1: `pt-PT`).
